assumptions: The recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a foreign-currency-denominated monetary item.
The question concerns the accounting in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk exposures that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a separate hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 provide a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting model for exchange differences on foreign-currency monetary items in consolidation when hedge accounting is not applied.
